trainingItems.title: Type of Application
trainingItems.description: Understand the different types of applications you can build with C# — console, desktop, web, and more. Learn where each type is used and how to choose the right one for your project.

codeChallenge.title: Hello, Console!
codeChallenge.prompt:
Print the following two lines exactly:
Hello, World!
I am learning C#.

testCases.expected:
Hello, World!
I am learning C#.

trainingItems.title: Intro
trainingItems.description: Get started with C# programming. Understand the basic structure of a C# program, learn how to write your first lines of code, and discover the tools and environment you will be working with.

codeChallenge.title: Full Name Greeter
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read two lines — the first name on the first line, and the last name on the second line.
Print: Hello, [FirstName] [LastName]!

trainingItems.title: Data Type
trainingItems.description: Explore the built-in data types in C# — int, double, bool, string, and more. Learn how to choose the right type for your data and how C# handles type conversion.

codeChallenge.title: Temperature Converter
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read a Celsius temperature as an integer.
Print the equivalent Fahrenheit value using the formula: F = C × 9 / 5 + 32

Example:
Input: 100
Output: 212

trainingItems.title: Variables
trainingItems.description: Learn how to declare, assign, and use variables to store and manage data in your programs. Understand naming conventions, scope, and how variables make your code flexible and reusable.

codeChallenge.title: Age in Days
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read the user's name on the first line and their age (integer) on the second line.
Print: [Name] is approximately [age * 365] days old.

Example:
Input: Alice (line 1), 25 (line 2)
Output: Alice is approximately 9125 days old.

trainingItems.title: String Manipulation
trainingItems.description: Master working with text in C# — concatenation, string interpolation, Length, Substring, Replace, Split, ToUpper, and ToLower. Strings are everywhere in programming.

codeChallenge.title: Count Vowels
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read one line of text.
Print the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u — case-insensitive) in that text.

Example:
Input: Hello
Output: 2

trainingItems.title: Arithmetic Operators
trainingItems.description: Use the arithmetic operators +, -, *, /, and % to perform mathematical calculations in C#. Learn about operator precedence and how to work with different numeric types.

codeChallenge.title: Simple Calculator
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read two integers on separate lines, then an operator (+, -, *, /) on the third line.
Print the result. Use integer division for /.
If dividing by zero, print: Error

Example:
Input: 10 (line 1), 3 (line 2), / (line 3)
Output: 3

trainingItems.title: User Input
trainingItems.description: Read input from the user via the console using Console.ReadLine(). Learn how to convert string input into the data type you need using int.Parse(), double.Parse(), and Convert methods.

codeChallenge.title: Rectangle Area and Perimeter
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read the width and height of a rectangle (integers) on separate lines.
Print the area on the first line and the perimeter on the second line.

Area = width × height
Perimeter = 2 × (width + height)

Example:
Input: 5 (line 1), 3 (line 2)
Output:
15
16

trainingItems.title: If Statements
trainingItems.description: Control the flow of your program with if, else if, and else blocks. Make decisions based on conditions and learn how conditional logic shapes how your program behaves.

codeChallenge.title: Grade Classifier
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read an integer score (0–100).
Print the corresponding letter grade:
- 90 to 100 → A
- 80 to 89  → B
- 70 to 79  → C
- 60 to 69  → D
- Below 60  → F

trainingItems.title: Logical Operators
trainingItems.description: Combine multiple conditions using && (AND), || (OR), and ! (NOT). Logical operators let you build complex decision logic from simple conditions in a clear and readable way.

codeChallenge.title: Leap Year Checker
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read a year (integer).
Print "Leap Year" if it is a leap year, or "Not a Leap Year" otherwise.

A year is a leap year if:
- Divisible by 4 AND not divisible by 100
- OR divisible by 400

Example:
Input: 2024
Output: Leap Year

trainingItems.title: While Loops
trainingItems.description: Repeat a block of code as long as a condition remains true using the while loop. Learn when to use while versus for loops, and how to avoid infinite loops with proper exit conditions.

codeChallenge.title: Accumulate Until Zero
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read integers one at a time until you read 0.
Print the sum of all numbers entered, not including 0.

Example:
Input: 5, 3, 2, 0
Output: 10

trainingItems.title: For Loops
trainingItems.description: Iterate a fixed number of times using the for loop. Understand the initialiser, condition, and iterator expression, and learn how to loop through sequences step by step.

codeChallenge.title: Multiplication Table
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read an integer N.
Print its multiplication table from 1 to 10, one result per line in the format:
N x i = result

Example (N=3):
3 x 1 = 3
3 x 2 = 6
...
3 x 10 = 30

trainingItems.title: Array
trainingItems.description: Store multiple values of the same type in a fixed-size array. Learn how to declare, initialise, and access array elements by index, and how to iterate over them with a loop.

codeChallenge.title: Find Max and Min
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read N on the first line, then N integers each on their own line.
Print the maximum value on the first line and the minimum value on the second line.

Example:
Input: 5, then 3 1 4 1 5
Output:
5
1

trainingItems.title: Foreach Loops
trainingItems.description: Iterate through every element in a collection cleanly with the foreach loop. It is safer and more readable than manual index loops when you do not need to modify elements during iteration.

codeChallenge.title: Count and Sum Even Numbers
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read N on the first line, then N integers each on their own line.
Print the count of even numbers on the first line and their sum on the second line.

Example:
Input: 5, then 1 2 3 4 5
Output:
2
6

trainingItems.title: Methods
trainingItems.description: Break your code into reusable, named blocks using methods. Learn how to define, call, and pass arguments to methods so your programs stay organised and easy to maintain.

codeChallenge.title: Palindrome Checker
codeChallenge.prompt:
Complete the IsPalindrome method so it returns true if the word is the same forwards and backwards (case-insensitive).
Read one word and print "Yes" or "No".

Example:
Input: racecar
Output: Yes

trainingItems.title: Return Keyword
trainingItems.description: Understand how the return keyword sends a value back from a method to its caller. Learn how return types work and how returning early from a method can simplify your control flow.

codeChallenge.title: FizzBuzz
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read an integer N.
For each number from 1 to N, print on its own line:
- "FizzBuzz" if divisible by both 3 and 5
- "Fizz" if divisible by 3 only
- "Buzz" if divisible by 5 only
- The number itself otherwise

testCases.expected:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz
7
8
Fizz
Buzz
11
Fizz
13
14
FizzBuzz

trainingItems.title: List
trainingItems.description: Use the flexible List<T> collection to store a dynamic number of items. Learn to Add, Remove, search with Contains, and sort a list — making it far more powerful than a plain array.

codeChallenge.title: Unique Sorted Numbers
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read N on the first line, then N integers each on their own line.
Print the unique values in ascending order, one per line.

Example:
Input: 5, then 3 1 3 2 1
Output:
1
2
3

trainingItems.title: Enums
trainingItems.description: Define a named set of constants with an enum type. Enums make your code more readable and type-safe when working with a fixed set of values such as days, seasons, or status codes.

codeChallenge.title: Season Finder
codeChallenge.prompt:
Complete the GetSeason method using the Season enum.
Read a month number (1–12) and print the season.
- Winter: December (12), January (1), February (2)
- Spring: March (3), April (4), May (5)
- Summer: June (6), July (7), August (8)
- Autumn: September (9), October (10), November (11)

trainingItems.title: How To Create A Windows Form App In .NET Framework Part 1
trainingItems.description: A step-by-step guide to creating your first Windows Forms project in .NET Framework using Visual Studio. Learn how to set up the solution, add controls to your form, and run your first desktop application.

trainingItems.title: How To Create A Windows Form App In .NET Framework Part 3
trainingItems.description: Continue building your Windows Forms application by adding pop-up dialogs using MessageBox and learning how to programmatically centre your application window on the screen at startup.

trainingItems.title: Intro
trainingItems.description: An introduction to the Tekla Open API — discover what it is, what structural workflows you can automate, and how to configure your Visual Studio project to start writing Tekla plugins.

trainingItems.title: Connect with Tekla Model
trainingItems.description: Learn how to establish a live connection from your C# application to a running Tekla Structures model using the Model class, and how to verify the connection status before accessing objects.

trainingItems.title: Create First Beam
trainingItems.description: Use the Tekla Open API to programmatically create a Beam object — define its start and end points and insert it into the active Tekla Structures model with a single method call.

trainingItems.title: Change Profile and Material Grade
trainingItems.description: Learn how to modify a beam's cross-section profile (e.g. HEA200) and material grade (e.g. S355) through the Tekla API after it has been inserted into the model.

trainingItems.title: Pick Two Points to Insert Beam
trainingItems.description: Use the Picker class to let the user interactively select two points in the Tekla 3D view. Use those two picked points as the start and end position of a new beam inserted via the API.

trainingItems.title: Read and Insert UDA Value
trainingItems.description: Read and write User Defined Attributes (UDAs) on Tekla model objects. UDAs are custom data fields used to store project-specific information — such as a part mark or fabrication status — directly on structural elements.

trainingItems.title: Github
trainingItems.description: Learn professional Git workflows — branching, committing, pushing, pull requests, and resolving merge conflicts. GitHub is an essential skill for team collaboration and managing code across projects.

trainingItems.title: Exception Handling
trainingItems.description: Write resilient C# code using try-catch-finally blocks to handle runtime errors gracefully. Learn about common exception types, how to throw custom exceptions, and best practices for error handling.

trainingItems.title: Debugging Code
trainingItems.description: Master debugging in Visual Studio — set breakpoints, step through code line by line, inspect variable values in the Watch and Locals windows, and systematically track down and fix logic errors.

trainingItems.title: BackgroundWorker / Async
trainingItems.description: Learn how to run long-running operations in the background without freezing the UI using BackgroundWorker and the modern async/await pattern. Essential for building responsive desktop applications.
